facts.subject: Council
facts.natural_language: The Council made a Recommendation on 2 June 2004 with a view to bringing the excessive deficit situation to an end.

source_locator.quote: In accordance with Article 104(7) of the Treaty, the Council made a Recommendation on 2 June 2004 addressed to the Netherlands with a view to bringing the excessive deficit situation to an end.

extra.model_quote: In accordance with Article 104(7) of the Treaty, the Council made a Recommendation on 2 June 2004 addressed to the Netherlands with a view to bringing the excessive deficit situation to an end.

facts.subject: Council Regulation (EC) No 1467/97
facts.natural_language: Council Regulation (EC) No 1467/97 established a deadline of 2005 at the latest for the correction of the excessive deficit.

source_locator.quote: Council Regulation (EC) No 1467/97 of 7 July 1997 on speeding up and clarifying the implementation of the excessive deficit procedure (2), established a deadline of 2005 at the latest for the correction of the excessive deficit.

extra.model_quote: Council Regulation (EC) No 1467/97 of 7 July 1997 on speeding up and clarifying the implementation of the excessive deficit procedure (2), established a deadline of 2005 at the latest for the correction of the excessive deficit.

facts.subject: Council
facts.natural_language: The Council Decision 2005/136/EC is to be abrogated.

source_locator.quote: In accordance with Article 104(12) of the Treaty, a Council Decision on the existence of an excessive deficit is to be abrogated when the excessive deficit in the Member State concerned has, in the view of the Council, been corrected.

extra.model_quote: In accordance with Article 104(12) of the Treaty, a Council Decision on the existence of an excessive deficit is to be abrogated when the excessive deficit in the Member State concerned has, in the view of the Council, been corrected.

facts.subject: European System of Integrated Economic Accounts
facts.object: Protocol on the excessive deficit procedure
facts.natural_language: The European System of Integrated Economic Accounts are defined in the Protocol on the excessive deficit procedure.

source_locator.quote: The definitions of ‘government’ and ‘deficit’ are laid down in the Protocol on the excessive deficit procedure by reference to the European System of Integrated Economic Accounts, second edition.

extra.model_quote: The definitions of ‘government’ and ‘deficit’ are laid down in the Protocol on the excessive deficit procedure by reference to the European System of Integrated Economic Accounts, second edition.

facts.subject: the Commission services
facts.object: a further decline in the general government deficit to 2,0 % of GDP in 2005
facts.natural_language: The Commission services project a further decline in the general government deficit to 2,0 % of GDP in 2005.

source_locator.quote: the general government deficit is estimated at 2,3 % of GDP in 2004, compared with 3,2 % in 2003. The outcome for 2004 is in compliance with the Council recommendation issued under Article 104(7) of the Treaty, particularly as regards the reduction of the government deficit below the reference value of 3 % of GDP by 2005 at the latest.

extra.model_quote: the general government deficit is estimated at 2,3 % of GDP in 2004, compared with 3,2 % in 2003. The outcome for 2004 is in compliance with the Council recommendation issued under Article 104(7) of the Treaty, particularly as regards the reduction of the government deficit below the reference value of 3 % of GDP by 2005 at the latest.

facts.subject: the Commission services
facts.object: a further fall in the deficit to 0,4 % of GDP in 2006
facts.natural_language: The Commission services project a further fall in the deficit to 0,4 % of GDP in 2006.

source_locator.quote: the budgetary measures taken are geared towards securing a lasting improvement in the general government balance. For 2005, the Commission services’ Spring 2005 forecast projects a further decline in the general government deficit to 2,0 % of GDP, largely in response to savings measures, mostly of a structural nature, adding up to 0,5 % of GDP.

extra.model_quote: the budgetary measures taken are geared towards securing a lasting improvement in the general government balance. For 2005, the Commission services’ Spring 2005 forecast projects a further decline in the general government deficit to 2,0 % of GDP, largely in response to savings measures, mostly of a structural nature, adding up to 0,5 % of GDP.

facts.subject: the Commission services
facts.object: the cyclically adjusted deficit to further decrease in 2005 by another 0,8 % of GDP, to 0,4 % of GDP
facts.natural_language: The Commission services project the cyclically adjusted deficit to further decrease in 2005 by another 0,8 % of GDP, to 0,4 % of GDP.

source_locator.quote: the budgetary consolidation will be sustained through a reduction in the cyclically adjusted deficit which is expected to reach close to balance after the excessive deficit has been corrected. In 2004, the cyclically adjusted deficit fell markedly to 1,2 % of GDP, compared to 2,0 % of GDP in 2003.

extra.model_quote: the budgetary consolidation will be sustained through a reduction in the cyclically adjusted deficit which is expected to reach close to balance after the excessive deficit has been corrected. In 2004, the cyclically adjusted deficit fell markedly to 1,2 % of GDP, compared to 2,0 % of GDP in 2003.

facts.subject: the Commission services
facts.object: the cyclically adjusted deficit to fall to 0,0 % of GDP in 2006
facts.natural_language: The Commission services project the cyclically adjusted deficit to fall to 0,0 % of GDP in 2006.

source_locator.quote: the budgetary consolidation will be sustained through a reduction in the cyclically adjusted deficit which is expected to reach close to balance after the excessive deficit has been corrected. In 2004, the cyclically adjusted deficit fell markedly to 1,2 % of GDP, compared to 2,0 % of GDP in 2003.

extra.model_quote: the budgetary consolidation will be sustained through a reduction in the cyclically adjusted deficit which is expected to reach close to balance after the excessive deficit has been corrected. In 2004, the cyclically adjusted deficit fell markedly to 1,2 % of GDP, compared to 2,0 % of GDP in 2003.

facts.subject: the government debt ratio
facts.object: the 60 % of GDP reference value in 2004
facts.natural_language: The government debt ratio was kept below the 60 % of GDP reference value in 2004.

source_locator.quote: according to the March 2005 excessive deficit procedure notification, the government debt ratio was kept below the 60 % of GDP reference value in 2004.

extra.model_quote: according to the March 2005 excessive deficit procedure notification, the government debt ratio was kept below the 60 % of GDP reference value in 2004.

facts.subject: the Council Recommendation of 2 June 2004 under Article 104(7)
facts.natural_language: The Council Recommendation of 2 June 2004 under Article 104(7) of the Treaty

source_locator.quote: under the terms of the recommendation addressed to the Netherlands on 2 June 2004

extra.model_quote: under the terms of the recommendation addressed to the Netherlands on 2 June 2004

facts.subject: Council Decision 2005/729/EC
facts.natural_language: Council Decision 2005/729/EC is addressed to the Kingdom of the Netherlands.

source_locator.quote: This Decision is addressed to the Kingdom of the Netherlands.

extra.model_quote: This Decision is addressed to the Kingdom of the Netherlands.
